# Parentless post-app-mount initVnode(true) auto-mount loses its round-trip reply

## Context

Filed per @neo-gpt's #14771 RA-1 AC-holder decision **B** (block PR #15062 on a fresh framework ticket). **Narrowed 2026-07-12 per @neo-gpt's intake V-B-A (`IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABJxLPOw`):** a current-runtime Neural Link control falsified the original "concurrent-ancestor" premise â€” the wedge reproduces with a fully-mounted **idle** viewport, so ancestor overlap is NOT the root cause. The owning bug is generalized to the parentless auto-mount round-trip; the Qt dock case is retained as the motivating symptom and a regression witness.

`#12946` (the original vdom-wedge) is closed â€” failure-containment precedent, but it did not pin this successful-create / lost-auto-mount-reply path. `#5038` defines parentless `createNeoInstance()` auto-mount as an intentional contract.

## The Problem (minimum reducer â€” idle viewport)

**A post-app-mount, parentless `initVnode(true)` loses its auto-mount round-trip, while the same instance's `initVnode(false)` succeeds.** No ancestor projection/update is required.

Minimum reducer (real browser, App-Worker, `neo-viewport-1` fully mounted + idle â€” @neo-gpt, session `d5afc497`):
1. Create a standalone `Neo.button.Base` with explicit `appName`, live `windowId`, `floating:true`, `parentId:'document.body'`.
2. `initVnode(false)` â†’ resolves normally, returns the expected vnode, viewport stays healthy.
3. `initVnode(true)` (equivalent standalone button) â†’ **times out**; the watchdog names the button itself after 5s. Live state: `isVdomUpdating:true, mounted:false, vnodeInitialized:false, vnode:null`.
4. Throughout, `neo-viewport-1` stays `isVdomUpdating:false, mounted:true` â€” unaffected.
5. Same wedge via the documented `Neo.worker.App#createNeoInstance()` parentless path with an explicit live `windowId`.

**Regression witness (the motivating symptom, not the root cause):** `examples/dashboard/dock` @ â‰¤620px â†’ 7 `main-tabs` overflow â†’ `dashboard.plugin.TabOverflow` eagerly mounts a floating overflow control to `document.body`; the App-Worker logs `vdom update wedged: "neo-viewport-1" â€¦ over 5000ms` (`VDomUpdate.mjs:453`). The original ticket mis-attributed this to ancestor concurrency; the idle reducer above is the true minimum.

## The Architectural Reality

- `Neo.manager.VDomUpdate` models component-tree relationships via parent IDs. A `parentId:'document.body'` component has **no ancestor-component flight** for the manager to merge or serialize against â€” so the "concurrent-ancestor collision" premise is structurally impossible, consistent with the idle-viewport reproduction.
- Parentless auto-mount takes the triangular special path in `src/worker/Manager.mjs#onWorkerMessage()`: `payload.autoMount` â†’ synthesized `insertNode` â†’ `handleDomUpdate()` â†’ Main delayed reply. `initVnode(false)` bypasses that DOM-apply leg and resolves.
- The failure is a **lost reply on the VDOM â†’ Main DOM-apply â†’ App triangle** for the parentless auto-mount round-trip.

## The Fix (prescription â€” evidence-first trace)

An evidence-first trace through the parentless auto-mount reply triangle: VDOM reply â†’ Main DOM apply (`handleDomUpdate` synthesized `insertNode`) â†’ App reply. Identify where the auto-mount round-trip reply is lost for a `parentId:'document.body'` instance. **Not** a manager-only concurrency fix (the concurrency premise is falsified) and **not** a plugin-side timing work-around.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root-caused: the lost-reply point on the parentless auto-mount triangle is identified (VDOM â†’ Main DOM apply â†’ App).
- [ ] `initVnode(false)` resolves AND `initVnode(true)` **mounts + settles** for a post-app-mount parentless `floating` control (both controls green).
- [ ] The idle-viewport standalone-control reducer is the pinned minimum regression.
- [ ] The original Qt concurrent-projection reducer (`dashboard.plugin.TabOverflow` @ â‰¤620px) passes after the narrow contract is fixed.

## Out of Scope

- The #14771 overflow-control affordance itself (stays blocked on this; RA-2 retained).
- Any plugin-side microtask / delay work-around.

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with` Epic #12986 (vdom-delta-stream contract). No ADR challenged or superseded.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-12T08:22:29Z

## [INTAKE_CLASSIFICATION] `needs-narrowing`

The bug is real and high-ROI, but a current-runtime Neural Link control falsifies the ticket's concurrency requirement and makes the manager-only prescription premature.

### Direct falsifier â€” 2026-07-12

Runtime freshness reported `current`. In App-Worker session `d5afc497-b9ec-4c96-ac68-69df8801cf48`, with `neo-viewport-1` fully mounted and idle:

1. Created a standalone `Neo.button.Base` with explicit `appName`, live `windowId`, `floating:true`, and `parentId:'document.body'`.
2. `initVnode(false)` resolved normally, returned the expected vnode, and left the viewport healthy.
3. On an equivalent standalone button, `initVnode(true)` timed out; the watchdog named the button itself after 5 seconds. Live state remained `isVdomUpdating:true`, `mounted:false`, `vnodeInitialized:false`, `vnode:null`.
4. Throughout that failure, `neo-viewport-1` remained `isVdomUpdating:false`, `mounted:true`, `vnodeInitialized:true`.
5. Repeating through the documented `Neo.worker.App#createNeoInstance()` parentless path with an explicit live `windowId` produced the same standalone-button wedge.
6. All probe instances were destroyed afterward; no `ticket-15083-*` component remains.

Therefore an ancestor projection/update is **not necessary** to reproduce the failure. The smaller invariant is: a post-app-mount parentless `initVnode(true)` loses its auto-mount round-trip, while the same instance's `initVnode(false)` succeeds.

### Source implications

- `Neo.manager.VDomUpdate` models component-tree relationships via parent IDs. A component whose `parentId` is `document.body` has no ancestor-component flight for the manager to merge or serialize against.
- Parentless auto-mount takes the triangular special path in `src/worker/Manager.mjs#onWorkerMessage()` (`payload.autoMount` â†’ synthesized `insertNode` â†’ `handleDomUpdate()` â†’ Main delayed reply), whereas `initVnode(false)` bypasses that DOM-apply leg and resolves.
- Historical `#5038` defines parentless `createNeoInstance()` auto-mount as an intentional contract. Closed `#12946` remains relevant failure-containment precedent, but it did not pin this successful-create / lost-auto-mount-reply path.

### Required ticket reshape

Please retain the Qt reducer as the motivating symptom, but generalize the owning bug/ACs to the parentless auto-mount round-trip:

- make the idle-viewport standalone control the minimum reducer;
- keep the ancestor-overlap case as an additional regression witness, not the root-cause premise;
- replace the manager-only prescription with an evidence-first trace through the VDOM reply â†’ Main DOM apply â†’ App reply triangle;
- require both controls: `initVnode(false)` resolves and `initVnode(true)` mounts + settles;
- require the original Qt concurrent-projection reducer to pass after the narrow contract is fixed.

Current intake state: `needs-narrowing`; no assignment, branch, or tracked edit. Once the body reflects the falsified minimum reducer, I can re-run intake and claim the corrected core lane.

Prior epic review remains satisfied: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/12986#issuecomment-4691574698

### V-B-A result

- **Problem validity:** confirmed on the current runtime. With `neo-viewport-1` mounted and idle, a parentless standalone control resolves `initVnode(false)` but wedges on `initVnode(true)`; the viewport itself remains idle and healthy. This removes ancestor concurrency as a necessary condition.
- **Architectural fit:** the rewritten ticket now owns the intentional parentless auto-mount contract from #5038 and traces the actual VDOM â†’ Main DOM-apply â†’ App reply triangle. The Qt overflow control remains a valuable end-to-end regression witness without being mistaken for the root cause.
- **Duplicate/successor sweep:** live issue searches for `parentless initVnode auto-mount` and `lost reply autoMount document.body` return only #15083; the matching PR search is empty. Closed #12946 remains containment precedent, not an active duplicate.
- **Negative-ROI alternatives rejected:** no plugin delay/microtask workaround; no manager-only concurrency patch before the lost-reply hop is identified.
- **Collision/ownership:** issue is open and unassigned; PR #15062 remains clean/open with this core defect as its RA-1 gate. Prior epic review for #12986 remains satisfied.

### Execution boundary

The implementation lane is to pin the idle reducer, instrument the parentless `payload.autoMount` path through `src/worker/Manager.mjs#onWorkerMessage()` and the Main delayed reply, identify the first missing correlation/reply hop, then repair the narrow owner. The Qt reducer must pass after the core control settles.

Classification: **code-ready**.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-12T08:57:06Z

## Investigation correction â€” reply is parked, not lost

Live paired controls narrowed the failure below VDom routing:

- In a visible Chrome 150 preview, a parentless component's `initVnode(true)` resolved and mounted.
- In the failing hidden Electron/Chrome 148 preview, both `initVnode(true)` and a direct harmless `Neo.worker.App.applyDeltas()` write remained unresolved.
- Reading Main state through the Appâ†’Main remote boundary showed `document.hidden === true`, `Neo.Main.running === true`, `Neo.Main.mode === 'write'`, and `Neo.Main.writeQueue.length === 11`.
- `Neo.Main` currently schedules all queue drains exclusively through `requestAnimationFrame()`. Hidden documents may suspend those callbacks, so DOM writes never apply and their reply promises remain parked behind the write queue.

This falsifies an auto-mount-specific routing loss. The implementation target is the Main render scheduler: retain animation-frame batching for visible documents, but schedule a task-backed drain while the document is hidden. The regression test should pin both branches and verify that the hidden write queue settles its reply.
